rulings.Case Title: HERSHEL HENRY STRONG VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Imo Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Oberta¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HERSHEL HENRY STRONG VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Tingue, Brown & Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. As to product identification, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and Mr. Walters¿ deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to asbestos content, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether the products attributable to defendant to which plaintiff was exposed contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to the portions of Mr. Oberta¿s declaration opining on asbestos content are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HERSHEL HENRY STRONG VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Warren Pumps, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Wunder¿s declaration are sustained only as to his statements that the packing and gaskets were asbestos and they are overruled as to all other statements. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Oberta¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
